title: cerastium vulgatum subsp. atlanticum
intro: Cerastium vulgatum subsp. atlanticum, also known as the Atlantic mouse-ear or sea mouse-ear, is a subspecies of the common mouse-ear chickweed plant (Cerastium vulgatum). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cerastium vulgatum subsp. atlanticum include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and soothe digestive issues, making it a useful herb for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in ointments and salves to treat skin conditions.
